questions.solution: Neither follows

questions.solution: 2nd from right

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The committee consists ____ ten members.
questions.solution: of

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The committee were divided in their opinions.
options: The committee has divided in their opinions.
The committee was divided in their opinions.
The committee is divided in its opinions.
The committee were divided in their opinions.
questions.solution: The committee were divided in their opinions.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The government appealed ____ the public to stay calm.
questions.solution: to

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He is more smarter than his brother.
options: He is more smarter than his brother.
He is smart than his brother.
He is smartest than his brother.
He is smarter than his brother.
questions.solution: He is smarter than his brother.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The project was completed ____ schedule.
questions.solution: ahead of

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The team is working on the project since May.
options: The team had been working on the project since May.
The team was working on the project since May.
The team has worked on the project since May.
The team has been working on the project since May.
questions.solution: The team has been working on the project since May.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The road is lined ____ trees on both sides.
questions.solution: with

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The professor gave a one-hour lecture.
options: The professor gave a one hour lecture.
The professor gave a one-hour lecture.
questions.solution: The professor gave a one-hour lecture.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She is proud ____ her achievements.
questions.solution: of

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She was angry on her friend for lying.
options: She was angry to her friend for lying.
She was angry on her friend for lying.
She was angry with her friend for lying.
She was angry at her friend for lying.
questions.solution: She was angry with her friend for lying.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: He is popular ____ his colleagues.
questions.solution: among

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She was angry on me for being late.
options: She was angry at me for being late.
She was angry upon me for being late.
She was angry on me for being late.
She was angry with me for being late.
questions.solution: She was angry with me for being late.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: He is ____ a nice person that everyone likes him.
questions.solution: such

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The data is being analysed.
options: The data has been being analysed.
questions.solution: The data are being analysed.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: He is ____ intelligent that he solves problems instantly.
questions.solution: so

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He has been working here since five years.
options: He has been working here for five years.
He has worked here since five years.
He worked here since five years.
He is working here since five years.
questions.solution: He has been working here for five years.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The city is known ____ its coal deposits.
questions.solution: for

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He is suffering from fever since last week.
options: He suffers from fever since last week.
He has been suffering from fever since last week.
He was suffering from fever since last week.
He is suffering from fever since last week.
questions.solution: He has been suffering from fever since last week.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of FERVENT:
questions.solution: Passionate

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The Earth's atmosphere is composed of several layers. Q: The troposphere is the lowest layer where weather occurs. R: Above it lies the stratosphere containing the ozone layer. S: The mesosphere and thermosphere extend further upward.
questions.solution: PRQS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: I would rather (A) / prefer tea (B) / than coffee (C) / in the morning. (D)
questions.solution: B - should be 'have tea'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She is ____ for her contribution to science.
questions.solution: renowned

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The scenery (A) / of the mountains (B) / are (C) / breathtaking. (D)
questions.solution: C - should be 'is'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The project was ____ due to lack of funds.
questions.solution: abandoned

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of PERILOUS:
questions.solution: Hazardous

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The time complexity of finding the median in an unsorted array is:
questions.solution: Quickselect (Hoare): average O(n), worst O(n²). Using median-of-medians: O(n).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: TCP congestion control uses which algorithm?
questions.solution: TCP: additive increase (congestion avoidance) + multiplicative decrease (on loss).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The decimal value of the hexadecimal number 1F₁₆ is:
questions.solution: 1F₁₆ = 31₁₀.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Technical debt:
options: The implied cost of additional rework caused by shortcuts or poor design choices
questions.solution: Technical debt: quick-and-dirty solutions → increased future maintenance cost.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about a singly linked list is correct?
options: Its size must be fixed at creation
It does not support O(1) random access by index
questions.solution: Linked lists require O(n) traversal to reach an arbitrary index — no random access.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The CNAME record in DNS:
options: Maps an alias to a canonical (real) domain name
questions.solution: CNAME: www.example.com → example.com (alias). Avoid CNAME at zone apex.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The binary representation of decimal 255 is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The YAGNI principle states:
options: Yes, Always Go with New Ideas
You Aren't Gonna Need It (don't add functionality until needed)
questions.solution: YAGNI: only implement what's required now, not what you anticipate needing later.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A graph with n vertices can have at most how many edges in a simple graph?
questions.solution: Complete graph K_n has n(n-1)/2 edges.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The RARP protocol:
options: Resolves MAC addresses to IP addresses (reverse of ARP)
questions.solution: RARP: used by diskless workstations at boot time (obsolete, replaced by DHCP/BOOTP).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For large n, which growth ordering is correct?
options: log n grows faster than n
questions.solution: Exponential 2ⁿ eventually dominates any polynomial like n².

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A use case diagram in UML shows:
options: Interactions between actors and the system
questions.solution: Use case = actor + system boundary + use cases (ovals).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 200×20 array of 8-byte elements occupies how much memory?
questions.solution: Size = rows×cols×bytes = 200×20×8 = 32000 B = 31.25 KB.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The MAC address size is:
questions.solution: MAC = 48 bits (e.g., 00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The base (radix) of the hexadecimal number system is:
questions.solution: Hexadecimal uses 16 symbols (0–9, A–F).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: INVEST in user stories stands for:
options: Independent, Negotiable, Valuable, Estimable, Small, Testable
Integrated, Necessary, Valuable, Easy, Simple, Traceable
Iterative, Non-functional, Verified, Estimated, Sized, Tested
questions.solution: INVEST: good user story characteristics (independent of others, testable, etc.).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 3, push 8, push 20, pop, push 30. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 20 then pop (removes 20), pushing 30 leaves 30 on top (LIFO).

figure.caption: Stack state

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: TCP connection termination uses:
options: FIN flags (4-way handshake: FIN, ACK, FIN, ACK)
questions.solution: Termination: each side sends FIN, receives ACK for the FIN. Half-close possible.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A nibble consists of:
questions.solution: Nibble = 4 bits (half a byte). Byte = 8 bits.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The CMMI maturity levels:
options: Initial (1), Managed (2), Defined (3), Quantitatively Managed (4), Optimizing (5)
questions.solution: CMMI: process maturity model. Level 1 = ad hoc. Level 5 = continuous process improvement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 3, push 9, push 20, pop, push 21. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 20 then pop (removes 20), pushing 21 leaves 21 on top (LIFO).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: VLAN trunking protocol (VTP):
options: Manages VLAN configuration across switches (distributes VLAN database)
questions.solution: VTP: Cisco proprietary. VTP server/client/transparent modes. Can cause VLAN deletion if misconfigured.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The decimal value of the binary number 1111₂ is:
questions.solution: 1111₂ = 15₁₀ (sum of place values).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Software testing levels:
questions.solution: Testing pyramid: unit (fast, many) → integration → system → acceptance (slow, few).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 7, push 8, push 14, pop, push 30. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 14 then pop (removes 14), pushing 30 leaves 30 on top (LIFO).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Given the network 192.168.1.0/26, the number of usable host addresses per subnet is:
questions.solution: /26 leaves 6 host bits ⇒ 2⁶ − 2 = 62 usable hosts (minus network and broadcast).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The binary addition 101 + 110 gives:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Definition of Done (DoD) in Scrum:
options: A checklist of criteria that must be met for a PBI to be considered complete
questions.solution: DoD: coded, tested (unit+integration), reviewed, documented, meets acceptance criteria.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 200×40 array of 4-byte elements occupies how much memory?
questions.solution: Size = rows×cols×bytes = 200×40×4 = 32000 B = 31.25 KB.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: TCP provides a service that is:
questions.solution: TCP uses handshakes, acknowledgements and retransmission for reliable, ordered delivery.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 2's complement of binary 1010 is:
questions.solution: 2's complement = 1's complement + 1 = 0101 + 1 = 0110.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: UML stands for:
questions.solution: UML = standard visual modeling language for software systems.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The worst-case lookup time of a hash table (all keys colliding) is:
questions.solution: If every key hashes to the same bucket, lookup degrades to scanning a list — O(n).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The TCP header has:
questions.solution: TCP: 20-byte fixed header + up to 40 bytes options. UDP: 8 bytes fixed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The octal representation of decimal 511 is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A software requirements specification (SRS) should be:
questions.solution: SRS characteristics: correct, complete, consistent, unambiguous, verifiable, modifiable, traceable.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The average search time in a chained hash table is:
questions.solution: Chaining: unsuccessful = α, successful = 1 + α/2 (assuming uniform hashing).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Session hijacking involves:
options: Stealing a user's session token/cookie to impersonate them
questions.solution: Session hijacking: steal session ID (via XSS, sniffing, fixation). Mitigated by HTTPS + HttpOnly + Secure flags.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Interpreted as an 8-bit two's-complement number, the bit pattern 11011100 equals:
questions.solution: MSB = 1 ⇒ negative; value = 220 − 256 = -36.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Agile methodology emphasizes:
options: Iterative development, customer collaboration, and responding to change
questions.solution: Agile Manifesto: individuals & interactions, working software, customer collaboration, responding to change.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The average-case time to search a well-distributed hash table is:
questions.solution: With a good hash function and low load factor, lookup is expected constant time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The OSI reference model has how many layers?
questions.solution: OSI: Physical, Data-link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An 6-bit register can represent how many distinct values?
questions.solution: 6 bits give 2^6 = 64 distinct patterns.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A state machine diagram shows:
options: States and transitions of an object in response to events
questions.solution: State machine: states (rounded rectangles) + transitions (arrows) triggered by events.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The leftist heap:
options: Supports merge in O(log n) (null path length property, left subtree heavier)
questions.solution: Leftist heap: dist(null) = 0, dist(node) = 1 + min(dist(left), dist(right)). Left dist ≥ right dist.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A router operates at which OSI layer?
questions.solution: Router = IP address-based routing (Layer 3).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The octal number 77₈ in decimal is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cohesion measures:
options: How closely the elements within a module are related
How modules depend on each other
The number of functions in a module
questions.solution: Cohesion (best→worst): functional, sequential, communicational, procedural, temporal, logical, coincidental.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A pairing heap:
options: A simple and efficient self-adjusting heap (merge in O(1), delete-min in O(log n) amortized)
questions.solution: Pairing heap: simplifies Fibonacci heap. Good practical performance (used in some C++ STL implementations).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The maximum data rate of HDMI 2.0 is:
questions.solution: HDMI 1.4 = 10.2 Gbps. HDMI 2.0 = 18 Gbps. HDMI 2.1 = 48 Gbps.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.solution: 10101 + 01110 = 100011 (1 carry at each position).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An activity diagram is similar to:
options: A flowchart (shows control flow or workflow)
questions.solution: Activity diagram: actions, decisions, forks, joins (workflow modeling).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The zigzag traversal of a binary tree is also called:
questions.solution: Spiral: alternating left-to-right and right-to-left at each level.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A /26 IPv4 subnet provides how many usable host addresses?
questions.solution: Host bits = 32 − 26 = 6; usable = 2^6 − 2 = 62.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The hexadecimal equivalent of decimal 255 is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Burndown chart shows:
options: Remaining work (y-axis) over time (x-axis) in a sprint or project
questions.solution: Burndown: ideal line (straight from start to zero) vs actual line (remaining effort each day).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A hash table with 100 slots holds 30 keys. Its load factor is:
questions.solution: Load factor α = n/m = 30/100 = 0.3.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DDoS attacks:
options: Overwhelm a target with traffic from multiple sources
questions.solution: DDoS = Distributed Denial of Service (botnet floods target).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The μ-recursive functions:
options: Are a subset of primitive recursive
Include the minimalization (search) operator, equivalent to Turing machines
questions.solution: μ-recursive: primitive recursive + minimization (unbounded search μ). Exact model of computability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 5, push 9, push 12, pop, push 21. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 12 then pop (removes 12), pushing 21 leaves 21 on top (LIFO).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The SQL GROUP BY clause:
options: Groups rows with the same values in specified columns (used with aggregate functions)
questions.solution: GROUP BY: SQL aggregate functions (COUNT, SUM, AVG, MAX, MIN). HAVING filters groups.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With a page size of 4 KB, the number of bits used for the page offset is:
questions.solution: 4KB = 2^12 bytes, so the offset field is 12 bits.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CFLs are closed under:
questions.solution: CFLs closed under: union, concat, Kleene star, substitution, homomorphism. Not closed under: intersection, complement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Branch prediction is used to:
questions.solution: Branch prediction guesses branch direction to avoid stalling.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FTP uses which ports for control and data?
questions.solution: FTP: port 21 for commands, port 20 for data transfer.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a 5-stage instruction pipeline, a data hazard arises when:
options: An instruction needs a result not yet produced by an earlier instruction
questions.solution: Data hazards occur from dependencies; they are mitigated by forwarding or stalls.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A foreign key:
options: References the primary key of another table
questions.solution: Foreign key = column(s) referencing PK of another table (referential integrity).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The hexadecimal number ABC in decimal is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 3, push 11, push 20, pop, push 30. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 20 then pop (removes 20), pushing 30 leaves 30 on top (LIFO).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 220 130" width="220" height="130"><rect x="80" y="20" width="60" height="24" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#dc2626"/><text x="110" y="37" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">30</text><rect x="80" y="44" width="60" height="24" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#2563eb"/><text x="110" y="61" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">11</text><rect x="80" y="68" width="60" height="24" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#2563eb"/><text x="110" y="85" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">3</text><line x1="78" y1="20" x2="78" y2="92" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="142" y1="20" x2="142" y2="92" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="78" y1="92" x2="142" y2="92" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="2"/><text x="150" y="30" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">← top</text></svg>

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Binary search on a sorted array of 256 elements needs at most how many comparisons?
questions.solution: Worst-case comparisons ≈ ⌈log₂ 256⌉ = 8.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Priority scheduling may suffer from:
questions.solution: Starvation solved by aging (gradually increase priority of waiting processes).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: User mode has:
options: Restricted access (must use system calls for kernel services)
questions.solution: User mode (ring 3): limited instruction set, virtual memory isolation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The spinlock is inefficient when:
options: The critical section is long (wastes CPU cycles)
questions.solution: Spinlock: CPU spins while waiting. Good for short CS. Bad for long CS (wastes CPU).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The 'nice' value in Linux affects:
questions.solution: nice value: -20 (highest priority) to +19 (lowest). Default = 0.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Shortest Job First (SJF) has:
questions.solution: SJF/SRTF: provably optimal avg waiting time. Preemptive variant = SRTF.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about processes and threads is INCORRECT?
options: Threads of the same process have separate heaps
Context switching between threads is cheaper than between processes
Each thread has its own stack
Threads share the process's code and data
questions.solution: Threads share the heap; only stacks and registers are per-thread.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The banker's algorithm is used for:
questions.solution: Banker's = deadlock avoidance (safe state checking).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 5, push 9, push 20, pop, push 30. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 20 then pop (removes 20), pushing 30 leaves 30 on top (LIFO).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 220 130" width="220" height="130"><rect x="80" y="20" width="60" height="24" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#dc2626"/><text x="110" y="37" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">30</text><rect x="80" y="44" width="60" height="24" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#2563eb"/><text x="110" y="61" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">9</text><rect x="80" y="68" width="60" height="24" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#2563eb"/><text x="110" y="85" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">5</text><line x1="78" y1="20" x2="78" y2="92" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="142" y1="20" x2="142" y2="92" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="78" y1="92" x2="142" y2="92" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="2"/><text x="150" y="30" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">← top</text></svg>

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A switch learns MAC addresses by:
options: Examining source MAC of incoming frames (builds CAM table)
questions.solution: Switch: forward/filter decisions based on MAC address table (CAM). Floods unknown unicast.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The time complexity of binary search for n=32 is:
questions.solution: Binary search halves the search space each step: O(log n) ≈ O(log 32).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A counting semaphore can be initialized to:
questions.solution: Counting sem: init to number of available resources.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: AVL tree rotation is needed when:
options: Balance factor becomes -2 or +2 after insertion
The tree has too many nodes
questions.solution: AVL: balance factor = height(left) - height(right). Must be -1, 0, or +1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: KMP (Knuth-Morris-Pratt) uses:
options: Prefix function π (longest proper prefix that is also suffix) to avoid backtracking
questions.solution: KMP: O(n+m) worst-case. π[i] = max k < i where P[0..k-1] = P[i-k+1..i]. No backtracking in text.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Dennard scaling:
options: As transistors shrink, power density stays constant (voltage and current scale with dimensions)
questions.solution: Dennard scaling (1974): voltage ∝ 1/k, current ∝ 1/k → power/area stays constant. Ended ~2006 (Dark Silicon).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 3, push 11, push 14, pop, push 25. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 14 then pop (removes 14), pushing 25 leaves 25 on top (LIFO).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 220 130" width="220" height="130"><rect x="80" y="20" width="60" height="24" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#dc2626"/><text x="110" y="37" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">25</text><rect x="80" y="44" width="60" height="24" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#2563eb"/><text x="110" y="61" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">11</text><rect x="80" y="68" width="60" height="24" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#2563eb"/><text x="110" y="85" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">3</text><line x1="78" y1="20" x2="78" y2="92" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="142" y1="20" x2="142" y2="92" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="78" y1="92" x2="142" y2="92" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="2"/><text x="150" y="30" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">← top</text></svg>
figure.caption: Stack state

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Instruction scheduling:
options: Reorders instructions to avoid pipeline stalls and improve ILP
questions.solution: Instruction scheduling: reorder independent instructions to separate dependent ones (hide latency).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A B-tree of order m has a maximum of ____ children per node:
questions.solution: B-tree order m: max m children, min ⌈m/2⌉ children (except root).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bridge operates at:
questions.solution: Bridge: connects two LAN segments, forwards frames based on MAC address. Modern bridges = switches.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Coupling measures:
options: The degree of interdependence between modules
questions.solution: Coupling (best→worst): data, stamp, control, external, common, content. Low coupling = good design.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Concurrency control using optimistic locking:
options: Assumes conflicts are rare; checks at commit time (version/timestamp)
questions.solution: Optimistic locking: read version, write only if version unchanged. Better for low contention.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Symbol table stores:
options: Information about identifiers (name, type, scope, memory location)
questions.solution: Symbol table: maps identifier to attributes (type, scope, offset, etc.).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A circular queue prevents:
questions.solution: Circular queue reuses empty slots, preventing false overflow.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 3, push 9, push 12, pop, push 25. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 12 then pop (removes 12), pushing 25 leaves 25 on top (LIFO).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 220 130" width="220" height="130"><rect x="80" y="20" width="60" height="24" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#dc2626"/><text x="110" y="37" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">25</text><rect x="80" y="44" width="60" height="24" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#2563eb"/><text x="110" y="61" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">9</text><rect x="80" y="68" width="60" height="24" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#2563eb"/><text x="110" y="85" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">3</text><line x1="78" y1="20" x2="78" y2="92" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="142" y1="20" x2="142" y2="92" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="78" y1="92" x2="142" y2="92" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="2"/><text x="150" y="30" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">← top</text></svg>

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A subquery in SQL is:
options: A query nested inside another query
questions.solution: Subquery (inner query) = nested SELECT inside outer SELECT/FROM/WHERE.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Namespaces in Linux:
options: Isolate and virtualize system resources (PID, network, mount, user, etc.)
questions.solution: Namespaces: 8 types (PID, net, mnt, uts, ipc, user, cgroup, time). Foundation of containers.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The parity bit is used for:
questions.solution: Parity = single-bit error detection (odd or even parity).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Dark silicon:
options: Only a fraction of a chip can run at full power simultaneously (due to power/thermal limits)
questions.solution: Dark Silicon: not all transistors can be powered simultaneously. Leads to specialized accelerators (DSP, GPU, ASIC).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: SDN (Software-Defined Networking):
options: Separates control plane from data plane (centralized controller)
questions.solution: SDN: OpenFlow protocol. Controller (e.g., ONOS, OpenDaylight) programs switch forwarding tables.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An interrupt vector is:
options: An array of pointers to ISRs (indexed by interrupt number)
questions.solution: Interrupt vector table (IVT): maps interrupt numbers to handler addresses.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A hash table with 50 slots holds 60 keys. Its load factor is:
questions.solution: Load factor α = n/m = 60/50 = 1.2.
